title: luzula subpilosa
intro: Luzula subpilosa, also known as the tufted woodrush or small woodrush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Juncaceae. It is native to North America and is often found growing in wet meadows and along streams. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Luzula subpilosa can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
